algorithms.title: Chemical Scene Response Sequence
algorithms.description: From detection through decontamination and toxidrome-based care.
steps: Detect: casualty cluster + one location + similar sudden symptoms (± odors, dead animals) = chemical release.
Stage uphill, upwind, upstream; no unprotected entry; establish hot/warm/cold zones.
Direct ambulatory casualties to the decon corridor; protected teams extract the rest.
Decontaminate: clothing off (removes most contamination), brush dry agents, then copious head-to-toe water.
Triage with SALT; give antidote auto-injectors during lifesaving interventions for the cholinergic toxidrome.

Treat by toxidrome in the cold zone: nerve agent → atropine (titrate to secretions) + pralidoxime + benzodiazepines; cyanide → hydroxocobalamin; pulmonary agents/vesicants → oxygen and supportive care.
Notify hospitals of agent and decon status; intercept self-presenters for decon outside the ED.
Observe exposures with delayed-onset potential (phosgene, chlorine, mustard).

algorithms.title: Casualty Decontamination Sequence
algorithms.description: Gross decontamination in the warm zone.
steps: Remove casualties from the source; prioritize symptomatic liquid exposures for immediate decon.
Remove all clothing and jewelry — bag and label as contaminated evidence.
Brush off any dry/powdered agent before water.
Flush with copious water head to toe (gentle soap for oily agents); protect airway; mind runoff.
Move the deconned casualty across the line into the cold zone for full assessment and care.
Never allow undeconned casualties or their clothing into clean areas or the ED.
